content.text:
Memory stage: EntityCanonicalizer v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Stage instructions:
        You are EntityCanonicalizer v1.

Goal:
Resolve entity mentions from the latest turns into canonical entities.

Return JSON object:
{
  "operations": [
    {
      "mention": "text",
      "action": "link_existing | create_new | add_alias | noop",
      "entity_id": "uuid-or-null",
      "new_entity": {
        "entity_type": "user | person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service | environment | other",
        "canonical_name": "text",
        "summary": "text-or-null"
      },
      "about_file_assertion": false,
      "alias_text": "text-or-null",
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "reason": "short explanation"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Prefer precision over recall.
- Do not merge distinct entities on weak evidence.
- Create new entities only when no candidate is a safe match.
- If a candidate safely matches the mention, use "link_existing" with that candidate id.
- Use "add_alias" only when the mention is a new surface form for a safe existing candidate.
- Canonical names are identity keys inside a namespace: the same canonical_name is treated as the same entity even if the entity_type differs.
- If distinct referents share the same surface form, disambiguate the canonical_name, for example "Java (programming language)" vs "Java (island)".
- If several candidates share a name, pick by entity type and summary; do not create another same-name entity just because the type differs.
- Prefer English for entity summaries; keep proper names, product names, repo names, and file names unchanged.
- Entity summaries must describe identity only, not mutable facts, current status, preferences, ownership, formats, fields, versions, or decisions. Put those facts into claims/notes instead.
- For USER first-person facts and preferences, resolve the user as the stable namespace-level USER entity named "User".
- For imported document/file_path/raw_url sources, the source path, URL, title, and section heading are source metadata. Do not create DOCUMENT, FILE, or URL-like entities merely to represent the imported source itself. Create a document/file entity only when the document content discusses that document/file as a durable domain object.
- For imported document sources, do not create FILE entities from incidental code references, stack traces, code blocks, file extensions, or paths such as "*.kt". Set action="noop" unless the text states a durable assertion about the file itself and later stages need that file as a claim subject or object.
- Set about_file_assertion=true only for FILE operations where the target text asserts something about that file itself. Set it false for non-FILE operations and incidental file-like mentions.
- Do not use raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" as entity_id. entity_id must be an existing candidate id, or null when creating a new entity.
- Use relevant retrieval context to resolve ambiguous target mentions such as pronouns, "it", "that", "first one", "second one", and "from that list".
- When TARGET_MESSAGE selects one item from a previously retrieved ordered list, create or link only the selected concrete entity so later stages can write the target claim.
- Never create chat-specific USER entities such as "User of chat:..."; a new chat is not a new user.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Detected raw mentions:
        No deterministic mention detector is available in this MVP.
Infer only concrete reusable referents from the latest turns.
For first-person USER statements, create or link a stable USER entity when claims need a subject.

        Candidate existing entities:
        - id=entity:<0>; type=CONCEPT; name=MemoryRun (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named MemoryRun (memory data model).
- id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Claim (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Claim (memory data model).
- id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Entity (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Entity (memory data model).
- id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=Episode (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Episode (memory data model).
- id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=Note (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Note (memory data model).
- id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Note maturity (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Note maturity (memory data model).
- id=entity:<6>; type=CONCEPT; name=Profile (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Profile (memory data model).
- id=entity:<7>; type=CONCEPT; name=Source (memory data model); aliases=none; summary=Concept named Source (memory data model).

        Relevant retrieval context for ambiguous mention resolution:
        - source external:document-section:8fa55469a066ca10b666d96d6b7c1f26: Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась
- source chat:<0> Memory ingestion source document. Treat the following as a pasted handoff data-model document, not as an immediate task.

File: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md

# 02. Data Model

## 1. Общая идея

Модель данных в `v3` должна одновременно поддерживать:

- typed memory
- lineage
- temporal truth
- reconcilable updates
- richer semantic context
- lifecycle / retention
- repairability

Поэтому модель делится на:

- **evidence layer** — `Source`
- **graph-shaped semantic layer** — `Entity`, `Claim`
- **rich intermediate layer** — `Note`
- **projections / operational memory** — `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`
- **audit layer** — `MemoryRun`

Плюс у `v3` есть два cross-cutting concern:

- `scope
[truncated 15083 chars]
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` run logs must not disappear silently.
- note hot-path:note:<0> v3 memory data model layered architecture; The `v3` memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by separating memory into layers: `Source` as evidence, `Entity`/`Claim` as graph-shaped semantics, `Note` as rich intermediate context, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode` as operational projections, and `MemoryRun` as audit.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> In the v3 memory data model, Profile is not the source of truth.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> In the v3 memory data model, controversial facts should be left in Claim or Note rather than pulled into Profile.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> In the v3 memory data model, if meaning is still too soft, Note should be preferred over a bad Claim.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see which model was used in a memory pipeline run.
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see exactly what was retrieved in a memory pipeline run.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see what budget was allocated in a memory pipeline run.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see where the memory pipeline broke.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see which operations were actually applied in a memory pipeline run.

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=02. Data Model|agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md|02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=02. Data Model agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке MARKDOWN Document title: 02. Data Model Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке Document lines: 619-628 ## 8. Модель данных в одной строке - `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом - `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста - `Task` = что должно быть сделано - `Episode` = чему система научилась - `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:8fa55469a066ca10b666d96d6b7c1f26] EXTERNAL

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась
